feature: Home page should support guest state controls, guest navigation, and login entry from the Person button

userStory: Home page should support guest state controls, guest navigation, and login entry from the Person button

As a guest user, I want the Home page to display the guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
This application is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the primary landing page for both unauthenticated users and authenticated users.
This workflow covers only the guest-state behavior of the Home page before login.
The Home page in this workflow should be treated as the guest state of the existing Home page, not as a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
GUEST HOME STATE When the application is launched and the user has not signed in, the Home page should load in guest state.

In guest state, the Home page displays the approved guest and shared controls, including: Person/Profile button Back button Notification button Home button Guest-accessible feature buttons Known guest-accessible feature buttons include: Customer signup button Clean survey button Site survey button GUEST-STATE EXPECTATIONS In guest state: The Person/Profile button should be visible and enabled.
Clicking the Person/Profile button should open the Login page or Login view.
Guest-accessible feature buttons should be visible and enabled.
Guest-accessible feature buttons should navigate to their supported destinations.
Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons should not be visible or should not be actionable.

Clicking the Person/Profile button should open the Login page or Login view.
The logged-in user name button should not be visible in guest state.

keywordNames: Verify Guest Home Controls Are Visible And Enabled
Verify Guest Feature Buttons Are Ready
Verify Home Page Remains In Guest State
Wait For Element To Be Ready
Open Home Page And Click User Button

keywords: Verify Guest Home Controls Are Visible And Enabled
Verify Guest Feature Buttons Are Ready
Verify Home Page Remains In Guest State

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page loads successfully in guest state and displays guest-accessible controls without showing authenticated user state information.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state shared Home controls are visible and enabled on initial page load
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are all visible and enabled in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify clicking the Person/Profile button from guest Home state opens the Login page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application opens the Login page or Login view from the Home page guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify clicking the Customer signup button from guest Home state opens the customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the guest Home page to the customer-search page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify clicking the Clean survey button from guest Home state opens the vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the guest Home page to the vehicle-survey page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify clicking the Site survey button from guest Home state opens the survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the guest Home page to the survey page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button remains enabled and responsive after returning to Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile button remains enabled after returning to the Home page and opens the Login page or Login view again without requiring a page refresh.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify shared Home shell controls remain visible in guest state after navigation back to Home
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button, Notification button, Home button, and Person/Profile button are visible and enabled again after returning to the Home guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest Home page can be refreshed without losing guest-state controls
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page reloads successfully in guest state and all approved guest-accessible controls remain visible and enabled after refresh.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify direct URL access to Home page always opens guest state when no authenticated session exists
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The SPA opens directly into the Home page guest state and does not redirect to an authenticated experience.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-accessible feature buttons remain actionable after opening and leaving Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Site survey button remains enabled and successfully opens the survey page after the user returns from the Login view without signing in.

validations: The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are all visible and enabled in guest state.
The Person/Profile button remains enabled after returning to the Home page and opens the Login page or Login view again without requiring a page refresh.
The Back button, Notification button, Home button, and Person/Profile button are visible and enabled again after returning to the Home guest state.
The Home button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.
The Notification button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.
The Back button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.
The Home page reloads successfully in guest state and all approved guest-accessible controls remain visible and enabled after refresh.
The Site survey button remains enabled and successfully opens the survey page after the user returns from the Login view without signing in.
transitions: The Home page loads successfully in guest state and displays guest-accessible controls without showing authenticated user state information.
The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are all visible and enabled in guest state.
The application opens the Login page or Login view from the Home page guest state.
The application navigates from the guest Home page to the customer-search page.
The application navigates from the guest Home page to the vehicle-survey page.
The application navigates from the guest Home page to the survey page.
The Person/Profile button remains enabled after returning to the Home page and opens the Login page or Login view again without requiring a page refresh.
The Back button, Notification button, Home button, and Person/Profile button are visible and enabled again after returning to the Home guest state.
The Home button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.
The Notification button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.
The Back button is visible, enabled, and displayed as part of the shared Home page shell controls in guest state.
The Home page reloads successfully in guest state and all approved guest-accessible controls remain visible and enabled after refresh.
The SPA opens directly into the Home page guest state and does not redirect to an authenticated experience.
The Site survey button remains enabled and successfully opens the survey page after the user returns from the Login view without signing in.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
